text.title: Dotfiles Management with GNU Stow Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of `Dotfiles Management with GNU Stow` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Repo MUST organize per-tool subdirs (bash/, vim/, tmux/) mirroring $HOME structure. Flat layout = REJECT.
rule.rationale: stow walks subdirs and creates symlinks per file; flat layout breaks the symlink model.

rule.statement: Public dotfiles repo MUST NOT contain secrets, API keys, or vault paths. Detection = REJECT.
rule.rationale: Public dotfile repos are commonly forked and scanned; one leaked token = compromised account.

rule.statement: Install script MUST be idempotent (re-run safe; no duplicate symlinks). Non-idempotent = REJECT.
rule.rationale: stow already is idempotent; install script must preserve that property when adding extras (apt installs, etc).

rule.statement: Install MUST detect existing target files (non-symlinks) and either back up or abort. Silent clobber = REJECT.
rule.rationale: `stow` aborts on conflict by default; the install script must propagate that and offer `--adopt` only with explicit user consent.

rule.statement: Machine-specific config MUST live in a separate subdir (e.g. `host-<name>/`) stowed last, overriding shared. Mixed = REJECT.
rule.rationale: Without separation, host-A and host-B share a single .bashrc and divergent settings collide.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume cycles and produce false confidence.
